opportunities.rationale: MongoDB dropped 27% on Mar 3 after softer FY27 guidance overshadowed a Q4 revenue beat (+27% YoY). Category B event-driven (guidance miss, not structural — Atlas cloud growth above 30% YoY intact). Price formed a stabilisation base at $258-272 over 12+ sessions (Mar 13: $260.50, Mar 17: $272.27, Mar 20: $261.16), holding the $258 crash low consistently. Jefferies and JPMorgan maintained Buy with PTs $310-325 (19-24% upside). 4/5 recovery signals: selling volume exhaustion, price holding above $258 for 12+ sessions, 2 major brokers maintained PTs, RSI recovered from sub-25 to ~38. R:R from $261.50 midpoint: stop $248, target $310, R:R 1:3.7. Invalidated below $248 on closing basis. Next earnings ~Jun 2026.

BioNTech fell 17% on Mar 10 after lowering COVID vaccine revenue guidance, masking a $2.1B+ oncology pipeline with multiple Phase 2/3 readouts in H1 2026. Category B event-driven (guidance miss on legacy COVID revenue, not structural). Price stabilised in the $89-91 range for 4+ sessions, holding $89 floor. Citi PT $130 implies 46% upside. 4/5 recovery signals: selling volume declining, RSI recovering from 28 toward 34, price closed above $89 crash low for 4+ consecutive sessions, analyst consensus Buy maintained. R:R from $89.50 midpoint: stop $84, target $103, R:R 1:2.5. Invalidated on daily close below $84. Oncology data readout is upside catalyst, not binary downside risk.

Dollar Tree crashed 22% on Feb 26 on Q4 miss and multi-year store restructuring guidance. Category B event-driven (execution miss with restructuring plan, not secular decline). Earnings reported Mar 16 — binary risk has passed. Price bounced to $114 post-earnings before retreating to $105.92 in broad market selloff, confirming a durable base above the $101 crash low. 4/5 recovery signals: post-earnings bounce to $114 confirmed institutional buy interest, price held above $101 crash low for 24+ days across multiple pullbacks, RSI recovered from 20 to ~40 range, stagflation regime drives consumers to trade down to discount retail. R:R from $105.50 midpoint: stop $98, target $122, R:R 1:2.2. Invalidated below $98.

Gold crashed 12.7% in 5 sessions (GLD $473 to $413.38) on USD strength + hawkish FOMC hold + forced liquidations. Weekend spot ~$4,497 shows early stabilisation 4 days post-crash. Category B event-driven (macro shock: rate repricing + USD surge, not structural gold thesis impairment). UPDATED entry zone to 415-430 — wait for confirmed stabilisation above $415 GLD (XAU ~$4,500). Condition: enter ONLY after 2 consecutive daily closes above GLD $415. Do NOT enter pre-PCE (Mar 28). Iran war safe-haven bid, stagflation backdrop, and OPEC+ catalyst remain intact. R:R from $422 midpoint to $455 target vs $395 stop = 1:2.1. Invalidated below $395 GLD (XAU ~$4,080). Size 1% risk max given unconfirmed stabilisation.
